description_long: This object is a large, rectangular block of stone, likely marble or limestone, with a roughly hewn texture on one side and smoother, more finished surfaces on the others. The front face features a Latin inscription, partially visible, suggesting historical or commemorative significance, possibly from the Roman era. The top surface is flat and shows some weathering and minor pitting, while the sides are mostly plain, with one side displaying a rough, chiseled finish. The stone is solid and heavy, designed to act as a guard stone or bollard, typically used to prevent vehicle encroachment or protect corners of buildings. Its substantial mass and hard surface make it highly resistant to impact and environmental wear. The overall coloration is a pale, off-white to light gray, with some discoloration and minor surface stains.
description: A large, pale stone block with a Latin inscription on the front, featuring both smooth and rough surfaces, used as a historical guard stone.
description_view_0: Front view showing a smooth surface with a partially visible Latin inscription and minor discoloration.
description_view_1: Side view with a plain, smooth surface and subtle natural stone veining, no visible markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a rough, chiseled texture, indicating unfinished or less visible side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a smooth, slightly uneven surface and some weathering marks.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near building corners, historical sites, or at the entrance to driveways to prevent vehicle intrusion.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on its broadest base, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible weathering and minor pitting on the top surface, with some discoloration and smoothing on the front and sides due to environmental exposure and possible contact with vehicles or foot traffic. The rough rear surface shows less wear, likely due to reduced exposure.
attribute_historical_markings: Latin inscription engraved on the front face, with classical Roman letterforms, partially visible and slightly eroded.
